key_insight: A memory-dependent fractured poroviscoelastic surface layer over a heterogeneous viscoelastic half-space strongly alters Love-wave dispersion, and the source waveform (Gaussian, Ricker, double-couple) significantly changes the predicted response.
engineering_relevance: This framework helps predict surface-motion amplification and structural demand in layered fractured media, which is relevant for seismic site response and underground/near-surface design.
method: Frac. viscoelastic constitutive laws using Riemann–Liouville derivatives
Fourier transform + Green’s function derivation of the dispersion relation
Hybrid Newton–Raphson solver for complex roots; synthetic seismograms and SDOF surface-response analysis
evidence_signal: The abstract reports synthetic seismograms, complex dispersion-root computation, and numerical simulations used to verify physical consistency and compare sensitivity to heterogeneity, fractional viscoelasticity, and porosity.
watch_item: The model is highly idealized and depends on parameter choices and numerical root-finding stability, so calibration against field or experimental data would be needed before practical use.
paper_title: Time and Frequency domain analysis of Love waves generated by Gaussian, Ricker and double couple seismic sources in a memory dependent fractured poroviscoelastic layer on a heterogeneous viscoelastic half-space
